cards.back: A brute force algorithm that tries all possible solutions to a problem, in this case, the Traveling Salesman Problem.
A classic problem in combinatorial optimization where the goal is to find the shortest possible tour that visits a set of cities and returns to the original city.
A subsection within Chapter 3 that deals with the implementation of the brute force approach to solve the Traveling Salesman Problem.
A supplementary material or appendix that provides additional information or details about the topic, in this case, the brute force approach for the Traveling Salesman Problem.
A method of solving a problem by systematically trying all possible solutions, in this case, the brute force approach is used to solve the Traveling Salesman Problem.
